title: TECHNICAL SUPPORT ENGINEER (INTEGRATIONS, QA, APIs) - US HOURS | G2 AWARD-WINNING US COMPANY Full Time
description:
Remote (Philippines) | Full-Time | Long-Term Role

You’re not looking to gig hop — you’re looking to build a career with a high-performing US-based team.

If you’re strong at troubleshooting, clear communication, and taking ownership, this role will feel right.

We’re hiring a Technical Support Engineer to help us solve real problems, improve systems, and grow into deeper technical responsibility over time.

We’re hiring a Technical Support Engineer to help us:

* resolve real customer issues
* improve QA and release confidence
* support go-lives and integrations
* reduce unnecessary interruptions to development
* strengthen how support, operations, and development work together

This is not entry-level support.
This is not a pure QA role.
This is not a junior developer role.

This is a hands-on technical role for someone who can work through ambiguity, think clearly, and move problems toward resolution without constant direction.

You do not need to be a full software engineer. But you do need to be comfortable with technical systems, structured data, debugging, and learning fast.

This role is a strong fit for someone from:

* technical support
* implementation support
* support engineering
* QA
* technical operations

ABOUT TOKEN OF TRUST

Token of Trust is a G2-recognized, award-winning platform that helps regulated ecommerce businesses verify consumer identities and comply with excise tax and reporting requirements.

We operate at the intersection of ecommerce, compliance, and trust — solving real-world problems for businesses that cannot afford to get it wrong.

We are a small, high-performing team. We value ownership, focus, continuous improvement, and delivering real results.

We are building a company where strong contributors can grow into deeper technical ownership over time — not just do a job, but become increasingly valuable as the company scales.

WHAT YOU’LL OWN

Technical Support Leadership (Primary Focus)

You will become the primary technical support person assigned to the Operations team for issues that require real troubleshooting discipline.

You will:

* triage, reproduce, and drive resolution of technical support issues
* distinguish between user error, configuration issues, integration problems, and product defects
* gather the right evidence before escalating to Development
* build and improve technical support playbooks so recurring issues can be delegated effectively
* personally drive challenging, ambiguous, or business-critical technical issues to closure
* communicate clearly with customers and internal teams during investigations
* become the trusted technical point of contact for operational issues that require strong judgment

QA and Release Confidence

You will be among the first to test new features and functionality so we can catch issues earlier and release with greater confidence.

You will:

* execute regression testing on key product flows
* write clean, reproducible bug reports with useful technical detail
* validate fixes before production release
* help reduce production surprises through disciplined testing and clear coverage
* build confidence that new functionality works across important use cases
* over time, help automate portions of regression testing and release validation

Go-Lives and Integration Enablement

You will help bring clients live successfully and, over time, act as the technical point person on go-lives and integration efforts.

You will:

* assist with client go-lives and implementation tasks
* configure plugins and integrations across platforms such as WordPress, WooCommerce, Shopify, and BigCommerce
* in some cases, make small technical customizations such as WordPress hooks, lightweight scripting, or small amounts of injected JavaScript
* participate in screen-share sessions with customers
* inspect settings, payloads, API behavior, and structured data to identify setup or integration issues
* over time, lead go-lives independently using well-understood technical playbooks, including cases that require light customization

Automation and Structured Operations

As the role grows, you will help identify and close the missing pieces required to automate chunks of workflow and make operations more efficient, less error-prone, and easier to diagnose.

You will:

* work with JSON, YAML, CSV, and other structured data used in our systems and processes
* use APIs, logs, internal tools, and technical artifacts to investigate issues and confirm behavior
* build or maintain light scripts, prompts, checklists, and internal artifacts that make the team more effective
* help codify recurring troubleshooting, QA, and go-live patterns so the organization gets stronger over time
* identify repetitive manual work, unclear handoffs, and avoidable sources of error, then help structure better ways of working
* contribute to a longer-term path into deeper technical operations, QA automation, or development-support work

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THE FIRST 90 DAYS

* technical issues are reproduced more quickly and escalated more cleanly
* known issues are increasingly handled through clear playbooks rather than repeated ad hoc investigation
* QA catches issues earlier
* go-lives become smoother
* Development spends less time on preventable support interruptions
* leadership is pulled into emergency situations less often because problems are identified sooner and clients are kept informed earlier
* repetitive operational work starts getting documented, structured, or automated

REQUIREMENTS

* strong written and spoken English
* availability to work 8 hours per day within US hours, with US Central overlap required
* experience in technical support, Level 2+ support, QA, implementation, or a similar troubleshooting-heavy role
* familiarity with ecommerce systems (e.g. WordPress/WooCommerce, Shopify, and/or BigCommerce)
* strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
* comfort working with JSON, YAML, CSV, and other structured technical information
* comfort reading API documentation and inspecting request/response behavior
* ability to write clear bug reports, technical notes, escalation summaries, and playbooks
* strong follow-through and self-direction
* reliable internet and a professional video-call environment
* familiarity using AI tools for drafting, analysis, or structured work, along with the judgment to verify correctness

PREFERRED

* experience using browser dev tools, Postman, curl, or similar debugging tools
* basic JavaScript, Python, PHP, or scripting ability
* experience validating integrations, webhooks, configuration, or data mappings
* experience with regression testing or QA process discipline

COMPENSATION

* starting at $900 to $1,200 USD per month depending on experience, hours, and demonstrated technical ability
* paid trial project before full offer
* long-term position

We are not trying to be the highest-paying listing for this type of role.

We are trying to be one of the best long-term opportunities for someone who wants to grow, take ownership, and become increasingly valuable to the team.

This role qualifies for our Growth and Incentive Program, where strong performance can lead to performance bonuses and permanent compensation increases as the company grows.

We also have a culture of continuous improvement. People who raise standards, build better playbooks, reduce repeat issues, improve workflows, increase automation, and make the company more effective can earn greater trust, responsibility, and upside over time.

We reward people who take ownership, raise standards, and deliver consistent results.

WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

This is not a ticket-processing role.

You will not be judged only by how many tickets you touch.

You will be valued for:

* solving real problems
* improving how the company operates
* reducing repeated issues
* creating clarity
* building systems and playbooks that scale

For the right person, this is a real growth path — not just a support seat.

Over time, strong performance here can grow into:

* deeper QA ownership
* more responsibility for go-lives and technical implementations
* support workflow automation
* technical operations leadership
* selected development-adjacent work

HOW TO APPLY

We take hiring seriously because we are protective of our high-performing team and this is a long-term role. Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be considered.

It is okay to use AI to help organize or polish your application. In fact, we expect you to. But your answers must still reflect your own experience, judgment, and thinking.

To confirm that you read this posting carefully, start your application with this exact phrase:

I Raise the Bar.

Please include all of the following:

1. Availability
Your exact US-hours availability, including your time zone and working hours.

2. Ownership story
In 5 to 8 sentences, describe one real technical support, QA, or integration issue you owned end-to-end. Tell us what happened, what you did, how you approached it, and what the outcome was.

3. Technical triage response
Respond to this scenario:

A customer reports that checkout verification is failing for most users on their live site, but the team cannot reproduce it in staging.

Please include:

* what you would check first
* the clarifying questions you would ask
* the likely categories of root cause you would consider
* what you would communicate back to the team or customer next

We read every serious application carefully.

If you take ownership, solve problems, communicate clearly, and want to grow, we would like to hear from you.

title: AI Systems & Automation Specialist Full Time
description:
Role Summary
We are seeking an AI Implementation Specialist to help drive our internal APEX initiative, focused on bringing practical AI into the business to improve speed, accuracy, reporting, process consistency, and operational execution.
This is a full-time role for someone who is highly organized, resourceful, detail-oriented, and excited about using AI tools to solve real business problems. This person does not need to be a traditional software engineer, but they do need to be highly capable with modern AI tools and able to use them to build workflows, improve processes, create useful outputs, and move projects forward quickly.
You will start by supporting quality control and execution inside our APEX roadmap, helping validate outputs, improve workflows, organize information, and assist in implementation. Over time, this role will expand into building and improving AI-enabled systems across multiple departments.
This is a role for a builder. We are not looking for someone who only talks about AI. We are looking for someone who can use it to produce real results inside a real business.
What You Will Be Working On
Examples of projects in this role may include:
• AI-assisted SOP creation, refinement, and maintenance
• KPI dashboards, scorecards, and reporting support
• Workflow automations
• Process mapping and documentation
• Data cleanup and structuring for AI use
• Internal knowledge organization
• Quality control of AI-generated outputs
• Building practical tools and workflows that improve team performance
• Supporting implementation of our APEX roadmap across departments
Primary Duties
• Help execute and support our internal APEX initiative for AI adoption and business improvement
• Review, validate, and improve AI-generated work for accuracy, usefulness, and consistency
• Build and refine workflows using AI tools to improve business processes
• Assist in documenting SOPs, workflows, standards, and process maps
• Help create reporting tools, metrics tracking systems, and operational scorecards
• Organize business information so it can be used more effectively by AI systems
• Collaborate with leadership and internal tea ---------- mbers to identify high-value opportunities for AI implementation
• Test ideas quickly, improve them, and turn them into repeatable systems
• Use good judgment to distinguish between useful output and low-quality AI-generated noise
• Support implementation projects across operations, admin, finance, customer service, and other business functions
• Maintain clear documentation of what is being built, why it matters, and how it should be used
• Continuously look for ways to improve efficiency, leverage, and execution
Tools and Systems You May Work With
Experience with all of these is not required, but comfort learning and working with tools like these is important:
• Claude Code
• Google Sheets / Excel
• ClickUp
• n8n
• Metabase
• SQL / Postgres / Supabase
• QuickBooks
• Other internal systems and operational tools
We will provide access to Claude Code for the person hired for this role.
Ideal Candidate Profile
We are looking for someone who is:
• Highly organized and detail-oriented
• Strong in written English
• Naturally proactive and resourceful
• Fast at learning new tools and systems
• Good at following structure while still thinking independently
• Comfortable working with ambiguity and figuring things out
• Process-minded and improvement-oriented
• Able to turn messy ideas into clean outputs
• Interested in business operations, not just technology
• Focused on usefulness, execution, and real-world results
• Curious, persistent, and capable of self-management
Essential Qualifications and Competencies
• Experience using AI tools in a practical business setting
• Ability to use AI to help create workflows, documentation, reports, or automations
• Strong written communication skills in English
• High attention to detail and strong quality-control instincts
• Ability to learn systems quickly and work across different business functions
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple tasks
• Good judgment and problem-solving ability
• Reliable follow-through and professional work habits
Preferred Qualifications
• Experience with workflow automation tools such as n8n
• Experience with spreadsheets, reporting, and KPI tracking
• Experience supporting SOPs, QA, process improvement, or operations
• Basic familiarity with APIs, SQL, databases, or structured business data (nice to have)
• Experience using AI to support implementation rather than just content generation
• Experience working with U.S.-based companies or remote teams (nice to have)
This Role Is Not For You If
• You only have theoretical interest in AI and no real examples of using it to improve work
• You rely on AI outputs without checking them for quality or accuracy
• Your written English is weak
• You struggle to follow structure, naming conventions, or process discipline
• You are not comfortable learning new software and systems quickly
• You need constant direction for every task
• You cannot work the required schedule consistently
• You are looking for a side job rather than a serious full-time role
Employment Details
Position Type: Full-time
This role requires full availability during assigned hours and cannot be combined with other employment.
Schedule: Monday through Friday, U.S. Central Time, 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM with a 1-hour break
Philippines equivalent timing will vary seasonally.
Compensation: PHP 45,000 - 75,000 monthly, paid weekly
Paid leave provided for recognized U.S. holidays when operations are suspended.
About Our Organization
Join Tri-State Tree Service, an industry leader with three decades of exceptional service throughout Pensacola, Florida and surrounding areas. Our TCIA accreditation places us among an exclusive group, with fewer than 1% of tree care companies achieving this distinction.
We believe in operational excellence, continuous improvement, and using technology to create real competitive advantage. We invest heavily in systems, equipment, process improvement, and innovation. This role is part of a larger initiative to build practical AI into the business in a way that creates real leverage and long-term value.
If you want to help build something meaningful, improve the way a real business operates, and grow with a company that is serious about execution, this is an excellent opportunity.
Application Requirements
To be considered, please complete all of the following:
1. Use AI Implementer as the subject line of your application
2. Submit your current resume
3. Provide a link to a video introduction of no more than 3 minutes explaining:
o your experience using AI tools in real work
o the most useful thing you have built or improved using AI
o why you are a strong fit for this position
4. Complete the short screening task below
Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.
Candidates must have a reliable computer, stable internet connectivity.
Screening Task
Please complete the following short task as part of your application:
Choose one business process that could be improved using AI. Examples:
• SOP creation or maintenance
• Quality control of team outputs
• KPI reporting
• Workflow documentation
• Customer service responses
• Task follow-up or internal accountability
Submit a short response that includes:
1. A brief description of the business problem
2. How you would use AI to improve it
3. What tools you would use
4. A simple example of the workflow
5. One risk or limitation of your approach and how you would handle it
Keep your answer practical and concise. We are not looking for the most technical answer. We are looking for clear thinking, good judgment, and real business usefulness.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer Full Time
description:
"We are looking for a detail-oriented team player with a passion for technology..." Yuck. Let's skip that.

We need a full stack engineer who can build anything we throw at them. Not a ticket-taker. Not someone who needs a Jira board and a standup to write a function. A builder. Someone who ships real products, solves real problems, and moves fast enough to make big teams jealous.

You should already be using AI tools to write and deploy code faster than anyone around you. If you're not prompting Cursor, Claude, or Copilot daily, you're already behind.

WE ARE NO PROBLEM
We're a young, fast-moving Australian team building performance-driven digital products and marketing for some of the hungriest automotive and real estate businesses in the country.

We don't just build websites. We build SaaS platforms, marketing tools, internal systems, and the infrastructure that holds it all together. Right now we're building marketing platforms for car dealers and real estate businesses. Both need serious engineering. APIs everywhere. Complex frontend. Database architecture. Payment systems. Notifications. The works.

On top of that, we run client websites, manage servers, and handle the full tech stack for the business. You'd be the technical backbone.

THE ROLE
This is a full stack, full ownership role. You will:
*Build and ship web applications from spec to production (Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, Tailwind CSS)
*Design and integrate APIs - vehicle data, payment gateways, CRM/DMS systems, SMS and email providers
*Own database architecture (PostgreSQL via Supabase - schema design, RLS, migrations, edge functions)
*Build frontend with modern React (TanStack Query, React Hook Form, Zod, Server Components)
*Create internal tools, dashboards, and admin panels
*Manage and update client websites (WordPress and custom)
*Handle VPS server admin - deployments, security, DNS, SSL, monitoring
*Domain management, email config, hosting migrations
*General IT/sysadmin tasks. You're the tech person. If it's technical, it lands on your desk.
*This is not a 'build one component and wait for code review' role. This is a results role.


WHO IS THIS FOR
You'll thrive here if:
*You've got 3+ years building and shipping real web applications
*You're strong in TypeScript, React/Next.js, and PostgreSQL
*You've deployed full stack apps end-to-end, not just worked on pieces
*You're comfortable on Linux servers, SSH, Git, CI/CD, and cloud platforms (Vercel, AWS, DigitalOcean, whatever)
*You already use AI coding tools daily (Cursor, Claude, Copilot) to ship faster than anyone else
*You've got a portfolio, a GitHub, side projects, something - proof you have a passion for building things even in your spare time
*Your English is strong, written and verbal. You'll be working directly with the founder every day.
*You don't need hand-holding
*You care more about shipping great products than job titles or org charts

BONUS POINTS IF YOU HAVE:
*Experience with Supabase, TanStack Query, Zod, React Hook Form
*WordPress dev and management experience
*IT admin / sysadmin / hosting experience (VPS, cPanel, Cloudflare, DNS)
*Marketing experience or exposure to the digital marketing world
*Experience with marketing APIs (Meta/Facebook Ads API, Google Ads API, analytics platforms, CRM integrations)
*Stripe or Xero API integration experience
*Exposure to automotive, real estate, or lead generation industries
*Experience building AI-powered features (LLM APIs, embeddings, vector search, chatbots)
*UI/UX design skills or Figma experience
*We care more about hunger, ability to learn, and what you've actually built than how many years you've been in a seat.

If you're a wizard who picks up new languages and frameworks overnight because you understand the fundamentals deeply enough that syntax is just detail - we want to talk to you.

WHAT WE'LL GIVE YOU
*Real products to build, not busywork
*Direct access to the founder. No middle management. No layers.
*Autonomy. We set the direction, you figure out how to get there.
*As many Cursor, Claude, and Codex credits as you could ever need
*A fast-moving environment where your work actually matters
*Flexibility to work from anywhere in the Philippines

WHAT WE WON'T GIVE YOU
*Micromanagement
*Excuses
*Comfort zones
*A bloated sprint process

WORKING HOURS
Full-time, Monday to Friday
9:00 AM - 6:00 PM Australian time (AEST/AEDT)
Remote - work from wherever you want in the Philippines
You need reliable internet and a backup connection. No exceptions.
Daily communication via WhatsApp and video calls
The Numbers
Here's the offer:
PHP 120, ---------- ,000/month (AUD ~$3,000/month)
Full-time contract, paid fortnightly
Compensation reviewed based on performance and contribution
HOW TO APPLY
Send us:
1. Link to Your CV/resume
2. A link to your portfolio, GitHub, or projects you've shipped. This is non-negotiable. No portfolio, no review.
3. A short note (2-3 sentences): What's the most interesting thing you've built recently? How are you using AI in your development workflow right now?

If you don't put effort into the application, don't bother.

***
If you want a slow, predictable, ticket-punching dev job - this isn't it.
If you want to build real products, move fast, and work with a team that rewards output - apply.

title: Full-Stack Software Engineer Full Time
description:
============================================================
OWN THE CLIMB
We Build AI Agents, Automations & Custom Software
----------
============================================================

FULL-STACK SOFTWARE ENGINEER
Next.js + Supabase + TypeScript
Remote | Full-Time (~40 hrs/week) | Compensation: Highly Competitive, starts at $2500 | Async-First

----

You will build AI-powered portals, CRM systems, and automation platforms for real businesses and see your work in production within days, not months. Our founder codes alongside you daily. Every project is a live client engagement across healthcare, legal, real estate, and finance.

We use Claude Code as a core engineering tool, structured project templates, and comprehensive development standards. Real autonomy, direct founder access, zero bureaucracy.


============================================================
STOP -- THIS ROLE IS NOT FOR YOU IF:
============================================================

x Your primary framework is Angular, Vue, Laravel, or Next.js Pages Router
x You have not built a production Next.js App Router app in the last 12 months
x You have never written raw SQL or designed a PostgreSQL schema
x You need step-by-step instructions to complete a feature
x You are looking for a maintenance, support, or legacy codebase role

We respect your skills. This role requires a specific stack and a builder mindset. If the above describes you, we encourage you to find a better fit. If not, keep reading.


============================================================
WHAT A REAL WEEK LOOKS LIKE
============================================================

MONDAY -- Spin up a new client portal from our Next.js + Supabase starter template. Define the schema, configure Row Level Security, push to Vercel.

WEDNESDAY -- Integrate the Claude API into an AI matching engine. Write Zod validation for the intake form. Debug an RLS policy leaking data across tenants.

FRIDAY -- The portal is in production. Real users are logging in. You move to the next build.

title: Senior Software Engineer – Data Infrastructure Full Time
description:
40 hours per week
9 AM–12 PM CST (10pm to 1am PHT) for meetings and then remaining hours will be FLEXIBLE

About the Role
We’re building a modern education infrastructure that’s AI-powered, insight-driven and student-centered. As our Senior Software Engineer – Data Infrastructure, you will play a foundational role in scaling our data systems to support advanced analytics, automation, and machine learning across the entire student lifecycle.
This role is both deeply technical and cross-functional: You’ll own the pipelines, integrations and backend systems that power real-time dashboards, AI tools and predictive intelligence while also building internal front-end tools to help staff interact with data, AI outputs and operational automations. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on clean architecture, robust automation, and building systems that just work: securely, reliably and at scale.

What You’ll Do
Data Integration & Engineering
- Design, build and maintain robust, secure data pipelines that ingest data from multiple external systems (Pipedrive, Disco, Google Classroom, Typeform, and more)
- Ensure high reliability through error handling, retries, status monitoring and logging
- Automate extraction, transformation and loading (ETL/ELT) of structured and semi-structured data using Python and SQL
- Normalize and enrich data to support dashboards, predictive models and staff tools

Infrastructure & Reliability
- Implement and enforce role-based access control, IAM policies and audit logs to meet SOC 2 and FERPA standards
- Build and monitor health checks and alerts for pipeline failures, data lags and schema mismatches
- Optimize our warehouse structure for performance, scalability and modular reuse across analytics and AI
- Set up job orchestration and scheduling using tools like Airflow, Prefect or AWS-native alternatives (e.g. Step Functions, EventBridge + Lambda)

Front-End Engineering & Tooling
- Develop internal-facing tools for interacting with AI systems, reviewing model outputs, approving automation actions or viewing student timelines
- Build lightweight tools in React, TypeScript, and TailwindCSS or use Streamlit for admin panels or dashboards
- Design intuitive workflows for staff-facing tools, annotation interfaces or moderation views
- Connect front-ends to cloud platforms (AWS, Snowflake), REST APIs, and internal services with appropriate authentication

Cross-Functional Collaboration
- Work closely with the Head of Data & AI Engineering, data analyst and product teams to prioritize technical work that supports critical operations
- Translate ambiguous data needs into scalable backend solutions
- Participate in data model design to ensure analytical and operational use cases are both supported and future-proof

Who You Are
Technical Expertise
- 5+ years of software engineering experience with a strong focus on backend or data systems
- Proven experience building data pipelines with Python, SQL, and cloud-native infrastructure (AWS preferred)
- Fluency in working with APIs and handling authentication, rate limits, pagination and error retries
- Comfortable working in AWS, building scalable, event-driven workflows with Lambda, Step Functions, S3 and Secrets Manager
- Familiarity with CI/CD, Docker, Git and monitoring tools (e.g., CloudWatch, Sentry, Prometheus)
- Experience with React + TypeScript and/or Streamlit for building internal tools or dashboards
- Proficiency in Snowflake, or similar cloud data warehouse (BigQuery, Redshift, etc.)
- Strong understanding of data security, compliance, and auditability (IAM, encryption, access logs, etc.)
- Experience with job orchestration tools like Airflow, Prefect, Dagster or AWS-native scheduling approaches

Communication & Collaboration
- Communicates clearly and effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
- Proactive about identifying blockers, surfacing risks and suggesting architecture improvements
- Able to document systems thoroughly for both internal use and compliance purposes
- Comfortable operating autonomously while staying aligned with the broader data strategy
- Committed to quality, documentation and building for long-term scale and auditability

Personal Attributes
- Systematic thinker: you take pride in designing durable, maintainable solutions
- Detail-oriented with a strong bias for clean, observable, production-ready systems
- Excited by mission-driven work and curious about how education, AI and automation intersect
- Comfortable working in a lean, remote, fast-moving environment

Success in This Role Looks Like
- You’ve built and deployed data pipelines that are reliable, fast and trusted by analysts, product teams and AI systems.
- The organization no longer waits on manual data pulls or ad hoc integrations - your work has enabled self-serve insights and model-ready data.
- Data pipelines are monitored, logged and compliant with best practices for privacy and security.
- Errors are rare and quickly recoverable, thanks to strong observability.
- Your infrastructure supports real-time student risk scoring, career automation and coaching dashboards - making it easier for every team to do their job better and faster.
- You are seen as a strategic partner who builds for the long term, not just the next task.

Why Join Us
- Build from the Ground Up: You’ll help design and scale the backend infrastructure that powers AI tools, dashboards and automations across our entire student journey.
- Tech with Purpose: Every pipeline you build and system you secure helps reduce student dropout risk, improve coaching quality, and streamline job placement - this is impact engineering.
- Autonomy with Clarity: Work in an environment who values clean architecture, fast iteration, and long-term durability and gives you the space to execute.
- Modern Tooling: Snowflake, Python, React, AWS, dbt, API integrations: bring your experience and help define the future of our data stack.
- Real Stakeholder Influence: Collaborate with cross-functional teams where engineering is not a service function but a strategic partner.
- Compliance & Scale: If you care about doing things right: IAM, auditability, observability, schema hygiene - this is the right home.
- Clear Docs, Clear Minds: We care about good documentation, thoughtful planning and building systems that last
- Remote-First and Human-Centered: We believe you do your best work when you’re supported, trusted and working on something meaningful.

About Coding Temple

Coding Temple exists to unlock potential.

We help people do the most meaningful work of their lives, and help companies build the teams that make that possible.
We prepare individuals with the skills, confidence, and real-world experience to succeed from day one. For employers, we deliver job-ready talent trained to meet the demands of modern work. Whether it’s a career change or a hiring challenge, we exist to close the gap between potential and opportunity—because when that happens, lives change and companies grow.

Our Vision
We envision a world where anyone, regardless of age, origin, or socioeconomic status, can achieve financial independence and professional fulfillment.

Our Mission
Our mission is to Empower Prosperity by delivering capabilities to professionals and organizations that drive economic growth.

We do this by creating a Virtuous Organization, a place where employees derive deep meaning from their work and gain increasing passion and energy from their colleagues, customers, students, and collective results. Together, we create an environment where each person can do their very best work.

Our core values guide us:
- Gratitude & Appreciation
- Dignity & Respect
- Support & Compassion
- Caring & Concern
- Meaningfulness & Purpose
- Inspiration & Positive Energy
- Forgiveness & Understanding
- Trust & Integrity

PS. Please don't apply if you have less than 5 years of experience. Thank you!
Please apply with your updated CV and include a cover letter summarizing your Python and SQL skills.

title: Senior Software Developer (AI / Full Stack) Full Time
description:
About Us
We're a fast-moving team that builds high-quality software for the music industry and at speed. We don't do bureaucracy. We don't do "good enough." We ship excellent work, fast — and we're looking for someone who thrives in that kind of environment.
Trust is the foundation of how we work. We hire people we believe in, give them a vision, and expect them to deliver. If you need someone looking over your shoulder to stay productive, this isn't the role for you. If you love building things that matter and holding yourself to a high bar — keep reading.

The Role
We're hiring a Senior Software Developer with deep experience in AI, prompt engineering, and full stack development. You'll be building, shipping, and iterating on AI-powered products and tools. You won't just be writing code — you'll be shaping how our products think, respond, and perform.

This is a hands-on building role. You'll work across the full stack, integrate and fine-tune AI models, design effective prompts and training pipelines, and make architectural decisions that directly impact our product.

What You'll Do
- Design and build full stack applications end-to-end (frontend, backend, APIs, databases)
- Develop, test, and optimize AI-powered features using large language models
- Write effective prompts, build prompt chains, and engineer reliable AI workflows
- Create and manage training data, fine-tuning pipelines, and evaluation frameworks
- Make smart technical decisions quickly — we move fast and expect you to keep pace
- Own your work from idea through deployment and iteration
- Collaborate directly with leadership to turn ideas into shipped products

What We're Looking For
- 5+ years of professional software development experience
- Strong full stack skills — comfortable across frontend (React, Next.js, or similar), backend (Node.js, Python, or similar), and databases (SQL and NoSQL)
- Hands-on AI experience — you've built real products or features using LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source models, etc.)
- Prompt engineering expertise — you understand how to design, test, and optimize prompts for production use cases
- AI training experience — familiarity with fine-tuning, RLHF, dataset curation, or model evaluation
- Speed without sacrificing quality — you write clean, maintainable code and you ship it fast
- Self-directed and reliable — you don't need to be managed. You see what needs to be done and you do it.
- Strong English communication skills — written and verbal

Bonus Points
- Experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, or AI agents
- Background in DevOps or cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or similar)
- Open-source contributions or a portfolio of shipped AI projects
- Experience working in startups or high-velocity teams

Why Join Us
- Speed and trust. We hire great people and get out of their way. No micromanagement, no pointless meetings.
- High standards. You'll work alongside people who care deeply about quality and craftsmanship.
- Growth. AI is moving fast. You'll be at the center of it, building things that push boundaries and will increase your skills.

How to Apply
Send us your resume and include the following:

A 1-minute video introduction (REQUIRED). Record a short video where you introduce yourself, walk us through what you've built as a software engineer, and tell us why you believe you should be chosen for this role over everyone else. Keep it to 1 minute — this is a test of how clearly and concisely you communicate. Upload it to YouTube (unlisted), Google Drive, or Loom and include the link in your application.

A link to your portfolio, GitHub, or examples of your work.

A brief summary of your hands-on experience with AI/LLMs and prompt engineering — tell us what you actually built, not general statements.

Applications without a video will not be reviewed.

We read every application. If you're the right fit, you'll hear from us quickly.

- Taylor

title: Executive Assistant - AI Consulting Business Operations (20-40hrs/week) Part Time
description:
Hi

My name is Howie! Thanks so much for reading this job posting. A little about me before jumping into the role: I'm a fun, friendly 30 year old software engineer living in California. I train Jiu Jitsu most days, travel a lot, do camping/road trips, am creative in both my work and personal life, and do amateur photo/videography that I'm going to start making content with. I live an active life and it's about to get more busy as I've just launched my consulting business and it's already moving fast. The rest of this job posting is dense and overwhelming so wanted to show that I was a real person first, not some business executive.

My ----------
My LinkedIn: ----------
Business page: ----------

UPDATE March 5: Thanks to everyone for their applications so far! I got flooded with work so will be following up this weekend. Will try my best to respond to everyone then, thanks

ABOUT THE ROLE
---------------

I run a consulting business based in San Francisco and Miami where I provide guidance on companies' operations, build any software they may need, and connect them to any new AI tools that would make them more efficient. Clients include tech startups, accounting firms, a law firm and individuals so far, so you'll get exposure to those industries. It's been one month but already I need help as I'm being quickly referred by my existing clients. My 3 month goal is to grow this from just me to an agency with other engineers, a designer, video editor, content/marketing. I need a sharp, resourceful assistant like you to be an extension of me in my personal life and for the business.

- The 20hrs/week for me will be a mix of my personal tasks and tasks for the business. If we end up needing more hours that's no problem for me, just estimating 20hrs for now.
- You'll also have the option to work 10hrs/week for my sister and her fiance's accounting firm that they run (or if you know anyone feel free to refer them)
- Then there will be frequent opportunities for you to do work directly for our clients as needed. For instance, if the law firm has a project they need help with. This can pay $7/hr.


WHY WORK WITH ME
-----------------

You won't be doing repetitive work. This is a dynamic, high-trust position. Here's what makes this worth your time:

You'll work with the latest AI tools - I use and build AI tools for a living. I live in Silicon Valley so I'm the first to hear about any new tools, and I get to meet a lot of the people building them. You'll be using those same tools daily. I hope we can work together forever but if not you'll come out of this role with hands-on experience that you can put on your resume or use to start your own business!

You'll learn my productivity systems - I work fast and efficiently. Together we'll create systems to get things done faster than 99% of the world. I invest in making us better - if you need something to do work more efficiently it's yours. If you want to develop a skill that will help the business go for it. The skills we build together will be beneficial for any future position.

You'll gain experience across multiple industries.

There's a real growth path - The right person's responsibility and pay will grow with the company.


SCHEDULE & COMPENSATION
------------------------

Hours: 20-40 hrs/week as stated above
Availability: Mostly on your own time but some work (like calling businesses) will need to be done during Pacific Time business hours: 9AM - 5PM PST. And I'm sometimes busy with clients during those hours so if you and I need to meet then it might be done outside of those hours e.g. 8AM or 6pm PST. So flexibility is a plus.
Rate: $5-7/hr USD (based on experience). Any ad hoc work for other clients billed at $7/hr.
Growth: Compensation reviews at 3 months and 6 months


WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING
---------------------

Business Tasks

- Researching and trialing new tools and services and reporting back on whether they're worth it
- Finding and vetting video editors for both business and personal content. Sourcing a designer for brand and visual identity work. Finding a front-end software engineer for project-based work. Identifying someone to help with brand strategy and content creation. A part-time junior software engineer
- Drafting and editing client emails and cold outreach in a real person's voice
- Industry research: What AI tools are being adopted in accounting? What does a mid-size law firm's tech stack look like?
- Setting up and optimizing the business profiles across platforms: Creating and optimizing profiles on Google Business Profile, LinkedIn Services, Clutch, G2, Yelp Business, and other relevant directories for consultants
- Conducting a digital presence audit - searching for the business across Google, LinkedIn, and other platforms, documenting where we rank, who appears above us, and identifying specific opportunities to improve visibility
- Seeing what other agencies do, copying them, maybe reaching out to work together or become friends
- Project manager - coordinating with me, clients and contractors
- Administration e.g. managing expenses in Quickbooks

Personal Tasks

- Managing schedules
- Making calls to US businesses on my behalf - banks, government agencies, insurance companies, service providers
- Emailing companies for customer support
- Email management: cleaning up inboxes, following up on deliveries
- Sourcing things I need to buy from Facebook Marketplace, Amazon, eBay
- Researching and booking travel arrangements: hotels, campgrounds, events, interesting stops, scenic routes, activities, events, restaurants
- Reaching out to professional services for things like moving, printing services, etc


TOOLS
------

You do NOT need experience with most of these. Having experience is a plus. What matters most is that you can pick up new tools quickly e.g. via YouTube tutorial. That's the skill I care about. Though some of these are very useful for any work you may do so even if you don't apply I highly recommend looking into them!

- Slack - communication, task assignment
- Loom - video recording. Besides Slack we'll be communicating through video more than anything
- Claude / ChatGPT (will ask to see your chat history because there are smart ways to communicate with these. This is the best way I can see how you think)

- Notion - documentation and SOPs

- Claude Cowork - desktop automation agent (big plus if you have experience here)
- Tango - step-by-step guides and SOP creation
- Toggl Track - time tracking and reporting
- CRM Tools e.g. Attio


QUALIFICATIONS
---------------

First thing's first - if your application sounds like it was generated by AI, I'll ignore it. That doesn't mean don't use AI to help write it - I'm using it to help me write this posting. But I'm editing it heavily so that it has my voice. A real voice. Your work will involve the same - clients don't want to get emails that sound like AI. So your application is the first test of this.

Must-haves:

- Strong written and spoken English. Reliable internet. Quiet environment to make calls.
- Available for a 2-3 hour window during US Pacific business hours (PST)
- Day to day flexibility would be really great. You can mostly do tasks whenever best for you but I might ask for things at any point during my day - like making a cool graphic for a last minute client presentation - and it would be nice if you were available.
- Available (some) Saturdays. Since I'm busy with clients during the week, my personal tasks end up on the weekends. Can work this out - maybe Sunday and one weekday off, and if we agree to work on off days we can do so at a higher rate.
- High agency - you take initiative and ownership, solve problems before being asked
- Attention to detail - you have high standards and double-check that all instructions were followed before completing a task
- Comfort with technology and a demonstrated ability to learn new tools independently
- Experience making phone calls to US businesses on behalf of a client

Strong pluses:

- Experience with any of the Business or Personal work listed above
- Strong background in computers e.g. IT
- Any content creation background (graphics, photoshop, video editing, social media)
- Background in marketing, SEO, branding, or helping grow a business's online presence
- Strong written and spoken Spanish
- Familiarity with business administration or bookkeeping
- Experience with any of the Business or Personal tasks listed above - please note them in your application!
- Trustworthy Facebook, LinkedIn profiles

Dream bonus (not expected):

- Experience with Zapier, n8n, Make, or similar automation tools
- Junior-level software engineering skills
- Experience as a travel agent or travel planning professionally


HOW TO APPLY
-------------

Please engage with the business page I just created - follow, like, comment
----------

Send a resume with references

Part 1: Video Introduction
Record a video introducing yourself, your experience in general, and your experience with any of the work or tools listed above. I'm big on "show vs tell" so if you have samples of your work or can show off some of your workflow that would be amazing. Even if you script this try to sound natural!

Part 2: Sample Task
Quickly find some software or AI consultant or consulting firm based in Miami and write up an analysis on them - could be their website, their services, their clients, their marketing. Then write them an email (don't send it) saying what interests you about them and how our businesses could potentially collaborate. Using AI is completely ok just remember in the end it has to sound human. Bonus points if this is all shown in the video, but can also just paste the write-up and email in your application.


WHAT HAPPENS NEXT
------------------

I'd like someone to be fully onboarded by March 16 (PST) but I'm tied up during the week this month so would be great if someone was available March 14 (Saturday) and/or March 15 (Sunday) just this once for onboarding. If these dates don't work for you let me know we'll work around it.

- March 9-13: Top 5-10 applicants will be invited to do a paid task ($5 USD) to evaluate your speed, how you figure things out, and your communication style.
- March 14: Top ~5 candidates will be invited to a video interview. To make sure we're a good fit! Will notify everyone of results on this day.
- March 15: Selected candidate starts onboarding.

I am always looking to help - if you make it to the final rounds where we meet but don't end up working together, I will keep your name in mind any time I hear of someone needing VA support.

I'm so so so so excited to work with you!!! Thank you

title: Facebook Media Buyer Any
description:
**WARNING: YOUR APPLICATION WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED IF YOU DON'T SUBMIT IN THE JOTFORM BELOW
---------- / ---------- Media Buyer (Facebook) — Full-Time (40 hrs/week)

Company: AT Media Partners
Location: Remote
Core Hours: 9:00am–5:00pm AEST (Monday–Friday)
Compensation: $700–$1,000 USD/month base + performance bonuses (up to $400/month)
Reporting To: Aaron Tang (Founder & CEO)


About the Role

As a Media Buyer, you will be responsible for launching, managing, and optimising paid advertising campaigns for our clients across Facebook Ads and Google Ads. Your mission is simple: drive consistent lead volume while hitting strong CPL targets.

This is local lead generation, not complex eCommerce. You’ll be running straightforward campaigns (radius targeting, lead forms, direct response) at scale — typically 40+ active campaigns — so efficiency and organisation matter.


Key Responsibilities

Campaign Setup & Launch
• Build and launch new campaigns for newly ---------- clients across Facebook & Google Ads
• Configure targeting, budgets, creatives, lead forms, tracking, and campaign structure

Daily Campaign Management
• Monitor performance daily and make smart optimisations
• Adjust budgets, pause underperformers, and scale winning ads
• Maintain clean account structure and ensure campaigns run smoothly

Cost Per Lead (CPL) Optimisation
• Continuously optimise to reduce CPL and increase lead volume
• Identify inefficiencies and implement performance improvements quickly

A/B Testing
• Test ad creatives, copy, audiences, placements, and campaign angles
• Use testing insights to improve results and prevent creative fatigue

Reporting
• Track and report key metrics such as CPL, CTR, CPC, frequency, conversion volume
• Provide clear updates to the internal team and support client reporting needs

Troubleshooting & Problem Solving
• Diagnose and fix underperformance (low CTR, high CPL, learning limitations)
• Resolve ad rejections, policy issues, and technical hiccups efficiently

Collaboration
• Work closely with the Ad Creative Team (images/videos) and CSMs (client requests + feedback)
• Collaborate with other media buyers to share insights and improve systems


What Success Looks Like (KPIs)

We measure success by results, not hours. Here’s what “winning” looks like:
• Average CPL: $11–$17
• Campaign Load: 40+ campaigns managed
• Ad Approval Rate: ? 95%
• Internal Response Time: < 2>
• Client Retention Support: Contribute to ? 11% churn by delivering consistent performance

If you consistently hit these targets, you can earn $900–$1,400+ per month (base + bonuses).

Who You’ll Work With

You’ll report directly to Aaron Tang (Founder & CEO) and collaborate with:
• Customer Success Managers (CSMs): handle client communication and relay requests/feedback
• Ad Creative Team: produces ad images and videos for your campaigns
• Other Media Buyers: collaborate on performance improvements and best practices

Our Clients

Our clients are:
• Blue-collar service businesses (plumbers, electricians, HVAC, roofing, etc.)
• Based in Australia and the USA
• Spending $2,000–$5,000/month on ads
• Expecting consistent lead flow (typically 20–50 leads/month)

What Makes This Role Different
• This is not an eCommerce role — no ROAS obsession, complex funnels, or multi-step conversion paths
• Local lead gen is straightforward, but you’ll manage it at scale
• You’ll have full autonomy over campaign management (no micromanagement)
• You can earn up to $400/month in bonuses for crushing CPL targets

Compensation

Base Salary (Full-Time)
• $700–$1,000 USD/month (based on experience)

Performance Bonuses (Monthly)

To qualify for bonuses, you must manage 40+ campaigns.

Average CPL Monthly Bonus

Example Monthly Earnings

• Exceptional performance: $1,300
• Elite performance: $1,400

Top performers consistently earn $1,200–$1,400/month.

Payment Terms
• Paid monthly in arrears (within 7 business days of invoice)
• Paid via Wise (Wise Business account required)
• You’ll invoice monthly with a breakdown of base + bonuses

Required Skills & Experience
• 1–2+ years running Facebook Ads and/or Google Ads (local lead gen preferred)
• Proven ability to hit strong CPLs (ideally $10–$20 CPL for service businesses)
• Strong understanding of core KPIs: CPL, CTR, CPC, frequency, conversion rate

title: SEO / SEM Specialist Full Time
description:
Full time career opportunity – diverse B2B/B2C Digital Sales & Marketing role in consumer products – growing responsibilities
A great long term career opportunity awaits a driven, agile, digital-savvy, hungry-to-learn individual who wants to take on growing responsibilities in using AI to drive digital sales and marketing for two exciting Australian consumer brands.
I own two established consumer brands (both have been around for over 50 years), historically operating in the wholesale space (800+ stockists). Over the last couple of years, the B2C channel has grown in importance and its simply too much workload for me to manage.
Your workload would consist of 2-3 hours per day of digital development work (enhancing websites functionalities and SEO improvements) another 1-2 hour on digital marketing initiatives (from EDMs to SEM) and the remainder on managing marketplace offerings and promotions. You will implement digital support projects (eg editing product images in Canva, generating AI images for digital ads, managing & optimising google ads, updating online marketplace listings on eBay, Iconic, Faire, Fieldfolio, Fruugo, Amazon) as well as responding to social media posts and posting pro-actively on social media (less than 2 hours per week). You are expected to explore how to use AI tools to make this work more efficient.
You already have experience in Digital Sales Support, consider yourself a fast learner and self-starter who is willing to take on additional responsibilities to support all sales activity through all our digital sales channels. You have a can-do attitude and are comfortable handling multiple tasks simultaneously.
Advanced Microsoft office experience is required, including having previously managed websites and online marketplaces such as eBay and Amazon. You have significant experience using Shopify and Ahrefs and have a good working knowledge with Canva and AI image generation. You have capability to manage SEO and Google Ads, are an expert in building backlinks, improving keywords and have some experience with AI tools such as Claude Cowork, Gemini and are keen to trial new tools in this space. You have experience managing marketplace sales such as Amazon and are driven by improving sales. Some website development experience would also be beneficial. Any challenges you face that are not defined by our procedures, you are willing to research yourself (eg youtube videos) to figure out how to do something.
The role is fulltime with salary increases over time in line with increased responsibilities. Hours are Monday to Friday and we honor most Filipino holidays except for All Saints and All Souls Day. Once you complete your 6 months’ probation, you are eligible for a 13th month salary.
This is a fantastic full-time career opportunity and the owner of the business is willing to invest a lot of time into your development. As such, if you are only after a short-term contract or prefer to continue doing some gig-work on the side, this role is not for you.

description:
Innovative global coffee brand inventing one-of-a-kind tools redefining how coffee is experienced. Seeking a Marketing Operator to own campaigns, launches, and cross-channel execution alongside founders.


ABOUT ICOSA BREWHOUSE:
ICOSA Brewhouse is a globally recognized specialty coffee brand redefining how coffee is experienced. We are shaping coffee culture around the world. ( ---------- / ---------- )

We design and invent one-of-a-kind tools that didn’t exist before — tools that elevate everyday rituals and introduce new ways of enjoying coffee.

From record-breaking Kickstarter launches to collaborations with world-class coffee professionals, our products are used by champions, in top cafés, and in homes in 100+ countries around the world.

We are a design-led, innovation-driven brand operating in a niche we are actively shaping. We build thoughtfully, test deliberately, and care deeply about quality — in both product and execution.

As we continue to innovate, launch new products, and grow, we are looking for a Senior Social Media & Marketing Manager to take ownership of how our work is brought to life across channels.

You won’t just publish content.

You’ll help shape how new products are introduced, how campaigns are structured, and how our global community experiences our brand — while ensuring execution remains disciplined, consistent, and scalable.



YOUR ROLE AND OPPORTUNITY ON THE TEAM:
This is a senior marketing operator role. Execution is central — but it is not a junior content posting role.

You will work directly with our Marketing Lead to:
• Own day-to-day marketing delivery across core channels
• Structure and execute campaigns from planning through rollout
• Translate ideas into clear, executable briefs
• Coordinate creative production across internal, outsourced, and AI-assisted workflows
• Strengthen how our marketing engine operates over time

You will see early product prototypes.
You will contribute to campaign discussions before launch.
You will help shape how innovation is presented to a global audience.

Execution is central to this role — but so is judgment, ownership, and the ability to think in campaigns rather than isolated posts.


What you’ll own:

(1) Marketing Execution Across Channels
• Own social media execution (Instagram and Facebook).
• Plan and manage content calendars aligned with brand positioning.
• Ensure consistent publishing and follow-through across platforms.
• Manage inbox and community engagement, including partnership or influencer communication if required.
• Execute email campaigns aligned with launches, promotions, and collaborations.

You ensure marketing execution remains structured, timely, and dependable.

(2) Campaign Rollout & Launch Execution
• Support and execute seasonal campaigns, product launches, collaborations, and promotional initiatives.
• Translate marketing objectives into structured creative briefs.
• Coordinate creative asset development across designers, freelancers, and AI-supported workflows.
• Oversee cohesive cross-channel rollout so messaging, visuals, and timing align across social, email, and paid efforts.
• Strengthen campaign structure and rollout processes over time.

You think in campaigns — not just content pieces.

(3) Creative Briefing & Quality Control
• Create clear, structured briefs for design and content production.
• Maintain high standards of aesthetic consistency, messaging clarity, and brand voice integrity across both human and AI-assisted workflows.
• Draft copy or guide AI-assisted content creation when needed, ensuring outputs remain on-brand and high quality.
• Review creative outputs for brand alignment before escalation to the Marketing Lead.
• Collaborate comfortably in tools such as Figma or similar platforms to support execution and alignment.

You don’t need to be a designer — but you should have strong creative judgment.

(4) Growth & Operational Development
• Identify opportunities to refine workflows and strengthen how our marketing engine operates.
• Lead or contribute to structured testing and rollout of new channels or initiatives as capacity allows.
• Over time, take increasing ownership of campaign structuring, cross-channel coordination, partnership campaign oversight, and creative alignment across performance-driven efforts.

As you demonstrate reliability and judgment, your scope expands meaningfully.


What success looks like:
• Social and email channels operate smoothly with consistent execution and strong brand alignment.
• Campaigns are rolled out in a coordinated, cross-channel manner without last-minute chaos.
• Creative briefs are structured, clear, and reduce friction across collaborators.
• Marketing execution no longer bottlenecks leadership attention.
• Early experiments in new channels or initiatives are tested in a structured way.

Over time, you become a trusted execution leader within the marketing team.


What This Role Is Not:
• Not a pure content creator role.
• Not a brand strategist role.
• Not a paid media buyer role.
• Not an administrative coordinator role.

This is a senior marketing operator position inside a growing global brand.



ABOUT YOU / WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR:
You are someone who:
• Understands how structured marketing campaigns come together.
• Can translate ideas into clear execution plans.
• Is comfortable owning delivery without hand-holding.
• Is comfortable reviewing performance data and using insights to inform execution decisions.
• Is strategic enough to think ahead — but practical enough to execute consistently.

You are strategic enough to think ahead — but practical enough to execute consistently.

Must have skills or experience:
• 4–6+ years of experience managing marketing and/or social for a brand.
• Experience executing structured marketing campaigns.
• Experience within D2C or product-based brands strongly preferred.
• Comfortable operating inside e-commerce environments.
• Highly detail-oriented and disciplined in follow-through.
• Strong creative judgment and ability to assess content quality and brand alignment.
• Comfort collaborating in design tools such as Figma.
• Comfortable working directly with founders in a small, fast-moving team.

Big pluses:
• Experience supporting product launches or crowdfunding campaigns.
• Experience creating, implementing, and refining marketing processes or building SOPs.
• Experience coordinating freelancers or creative collaborators.
• Familiarity with Klaviyo or similar email platforms.
• Exposure to AI-assisted marketing workflows.



WHY JOIN US:
At ICOSA Brewhouse, you will:
• Work directly with founders who are deeply involved in product design, positioning, and global campaigns.
• Operate close to innovation — seeing products and launches before they are introduced to the world.
• Contribute to campaigns that reach a global coffee audience.
• Have real ownership inside a small, high-performing team where your work is visible and impactful.
• Help shape coffee culture around the world.

We are a fully remote team — but we operate with structure and discipline.

You’ll be joining a close-knit group focused on building something meaningful with collaboration and accountability. There is no office politics. No unnecessary hierarchy. Just people committed to high standards.

We care deeply about growth — not just as a company, but as individuals. As we expand, there are real opportunities to take on broader ownership, lead initiatives, and shape how our marketing evolves.

If you want a role where:
• Your ideas are heard.
• Your execution matters.
• Your scope can grow with your capability.
You will feel at home here.



COMPENSATION AND WORKING STRUCTURE
Full time: 40 hours/week
Compensation range: $1,100-$1,800 (based on relevant experience and demonstrated capability)
Up to 16 days of paid time off annually
Asia time zone working hours required

title: Go High Level funnel and automation expert Part Time
description:
About the Project
I run an IELTS test preparation brand (IELTS Ebook Empire) selling digital ebooks, mock test bundles, and paid 1:1 coaching sessions to IELTS candidates targeting Band 7+. I need an experienced GoHighLevel specialist to build a complete, multi-branch quiz funnel system natively inside GHL — including all funnel pages, surveys, two-step order forms, workflow automations, email sequence triggers, calendar booking, and Stripe payment integration.
A comprehensive build specification document has already been written. It contains the full funnel architecture, every page’s copy, design direction, element-by-element layout specs, tag architecture, workflow logic, and GHL AI Build prompts. Your job is to execute this spec inside GoHighLevel, not to write copy or design from scratch. The thinking is done — I need a builder who can turn a detailed blueprint into a live, fully functioning funnel system.

What You Will Build Inside GoHighLevel
The system is a quiz-to-sale funnel that captures IELTS candidates via a single quiz entry point, routes them to one of four skill-specific branches (Writing, Reading, Speaking, Listening), and guides each lead through a price ladder: free lead magnet, $9 tripwire ebook, $37–$47 mock test upsell bundle, and paid 1:1 coaching session. Everything lives natively inside GHL.
Asset Inventory — What Gets Built
GHL Asset
Qty
Description
1 Quiz Landing Page
1 Standalone GHL funnel page with full-screen navy hero section, social proof strip, and CTA. Hosts the embedded GHL Survey.
1 GHL Survey
4 2-slide survey: Slide 1 is a single-choice routing question (4 answer options). Slide 2 captures first name and email. Conditional logic redirects each answer to its branch funnel.
4 Branch Lead Magnet Funnels. One per IELTS skill. Each has 4–5 steps: confirmation + diagnosis, problem naming, tripwire offer introduction, offer card with GHL two-step order form, and delivery reminder page.
4. Branch Upsell Funnels One per skill. Each has 3 steps: celebration bridge, upsell offer card with GHL two-step order form, and purchase confirmation.
4. Branch Coaching Funnels. One per skill. Each has 4 steps: authority hero, session details, branch-specific focus copy, and calendar booking with price display.
8 GHL Two-Step Order Forms. One on each tripwire offer page ($9) and one on each upsell page ($37–$47). Connected to Stripe.
1. GHL Calendar Coaching session calendar with 45-minute slots, auto-timezone detection, embedded on all 4 coaching funnel Step 4 pages.
5–8 GHL Workflows. Survey submit workflow (4-branch If/Else), tripwire purchase workflow, upsell purchase workflow, coaching booking workflow, and cold lead re-engagement workflow.
17 GHL Tags. Pre-defined tag set for lead segmentation, buyer tracking, and automation triggers across all branches.
6 GHL Custom Fields. Contact fields for branch tracking, UTM parameters, funnel source, and product purchase history.
GHL Pipeline
2 IELTS Leads pipeline (4 stages) and Coaching pipeline (3 stages).
8 Stripe Products 4x $9 tripwire products + 4x $37–$47 upsell products created in Stripe and linked to GHL order forms.
1 Meta Pixel Integration
Base pixel code installed across all funnels with event-specific triggers (Lead, ViewContent, InitiateCheckout, Purchase, Schedule).

Total Funnel Pages
Quiz landing page: 1 page
Lead magnet funnels: 4 branches × 5 steps = 20 pages
Upsell funnels: 4 branches × 3 steps = 12 pages
Coaching funnels: 4 branches × 4 steps = 16 pages

Detailed Scope of Work
Below is a step-by-step breakdown of everything included in this engagement. Each task references a specific section of the build spec document you will receive.
Phase 1: GHL Account Setup & Foundation
Before building any pages, you will configure the backend infrastructure that powers the entire system.
Create 17 GHL tags across four categories: lead tags (4), tripwire buyer tags (4), core upsell buyer tags (4), coaching booked tags (4), and one cold-lead re-engagement tag. Exact tag names are defined in the spec.
Create 6 custom contact fields for branch tracking (ielts_branch), funnel source attribution (funnel_source), UTM passthrough (utm_source, utm_campaign), and product purchase tracking (tripwire_product, core_product).
Create 2 GHL pipelines: IELTS Leads (stages: New Lead ? Tripwire Buyer ? Core Buyer ? Coaching Booked) and Coaching (stages: Booked ? Pre-Session ? Completed).
Connect Stripe to the GHL account. Create 8 products in Stripe (4 tripwire products at $9 each, 4 upsell products at $37–$47 each) and link them to GHL.
Set up GHL Calendar for coaching sessions: 45-minute appointment slots, auto-timezone detection, and booking confirmation email.
Install Google Fonts via custom CSS in each funnel’s header code (Playfair Display for headlines, Inter for body, Source Code Pro for labels). CSS import snippet is provided in the spec.
Phase 2: Shared Quiz Entry Point
The entry funnel is the single page all paid ad traffic lands on.
Build the quiz landing page as a GHL funnel with one step. Full-screen navy hero section with eyebrow label, three-line headline, sub-headline, social proof strip, teal CTA button, and trust micro-copy. All copy is provided word-for-word in the spec.
Create the GHL Survey with two slides. Slide 1: single-choice routing question with four card-style answer options (Writing, Reading, Speaking, Listening), each with a sub-label. Auto-advance on selection. Slide 2: first name and email capture with personalised headline and teal submit button.
Configure survey conditional logic: four redirect rules that route each answer to its branch-specific result funnel URL.
Embed the survey on the landing page below the hero section.
Install Meta Pixel base code on the funnel and fire the Lead event on survey submission.
Phase 3: Branch Lead Magnet Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)
Build the Writing branch first as the template funnel, then clone and adapt for Reading, Speaking, and Listening.
Build the Writing Lead Magnet funnel with 4–5 steps. Step 1: navy confirmation screen with success badge, headline, body copy, pull quote (gold left border), and scroll CTA. Step 2: cream problem-naming section with diagnostic headline, body, 3-stat strip, and system description. Step 3: navy tripwire introduction with gold eyebrow, offer bridge copy, and red urgency line. Step 4: cream offer card with product title, $9/$27 price display, 6-item teal-tick checklist, FOMO notice, GHL two-step order form, trust line, and skip link. Step 5 (skip destination): teal-pale delivery reminder.
Add confetti script to Step 1 using the JavaScript snippet provided in the spec (canvas-confetti library).
Configure the GHL two-step order form on Step 4: connect to the $9 Writing Stripe product. Pre-fill contact info from the survey submission. On successful payment, redirect to the Writing upsell funnel.
Clone the Writing funnel three times. Rename each clone to Reading, Speaking, and Listening. Swap all copy, product names, checklist items, stats, and pull quotes using the branch-specific content provided in the spec document. Update each order form to point to the correct Stripe product.
Phase 4: Branch Upsell Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)
Each upsell funnel loads immediately after a $9 tripwire purchase.
Build the Writing Upsell funnel with 3 steps. Step 1: navy celebration hero with gold badge, headline, italic teal sub-headline, and bridge copy. Step 2: cream offer card with product title ($47 Mock Test + Examiner Feedback Bundle), $47/$97 price display, 6-item checklist, urgency box, GHL two-step order form, and skip link. Step 3: teal-pale purchase confirmation with delivery instructions and 3-step next actions.
Configure the GHL two-step order form on Step 2: connect to the $47 Writing Stripe product.
Clone three times for Reading ($37), Speaking ($47), and Listening ($37). Swap all copy and reconnect order forms to correct Stripe products.
Phase 5: Branch Coaching Appointment Funnels (Build 1, Clone 3)
Each coaching funnel is a premium, paid 1:1 session booking flow.
Build the Writing Coaching funnel with 4 steps. Step 1: navy authority hero with gold eyebrow, headline, PhD credentials body, 3-item credential strip, and CTA. Step 2: cream session details with 3 numbered cards (Before/During/After), format info, and CTA. Step 3: cream branch-specific focus with Writing-specific headline and body copy. Step 4: navy calendar and pricing section with gold price display ($97), embedded GHL Calendar widget, preparation instructions, teal-pale reassurance box, and confirm button.
Embed the GHL Calendar on Step 4 and configure timezone auto-detection.
Clone three times for Reading, Speaking, and Listening. Only Step 3 copy changes between branches — the rest is identical.
Phase 6: GHL Workflow Automations
Build the workflow layer that connects every funnel action to the correct tag, sequence, and pipeline movement.
Workflow 1 — Survey Submit: Triggered when the IELTS Quiz Survey is submitted. Uses If/Else branching to check the ielts_branch custom field. For each branch: applies the correct lead tag, adds the contact to the IELTS Leads pipeline (New Lead stage), and starts Sequence A for that branch.
Workflow 2 — Tripwire Purchase: Triggered on $9 order form payment. Applies the correct tripwire-buyer tag, sets the tripwire_product custom field, redirects the buyer to their branch upsell funnel, and starts Sequence B.
Workflow 3 — Upsell Purchase: Triggered on $37–$47 order form payment. Applies the correct core-buyer tag, sets the core_product custom field, moves the contact to Core Buyer pipeline stage, and starts Sequence C.
Workflow 4 — Coaching Booking: Triggered on GHL Calendar booking confirmation. Applies the coaching-booked tag, moves to the Coaching pipeline (Booked stage), sends a confirmation email with pre-session instructions, and sends an internal notification.
Workflow 5 — Cold Lead Re-engagement: Triggered when a contact has no email opens for 30 days. Adds the cold-30d tag. Starts a 3-email re-engagement mini-sequence.
Phase 7: Meta Pixel Events
Configure event-specific pixel fires at each conversion point across all funnels.
Event
Trigger Point
Lead
Survey submission (all branches)
ViewContent
Upsell offer card visible
InitiateCheckout
CTA click on any order form
Purchase ($9)
Tripwire order form payment confirmed
Purchase ($37–$47)
Upsell order form payment confirmed
Schedule
Calendar booking confirmed

Phase 8: Testing & Launch
End-to-end testing: Complete a full test transaction through all 4 branches — quiz ? result page ? $9 purchase ? upsell ? coaching booking. Verify each workflow fires correctly, tags are applied, pipeline stages update, and payment processes through Stripe.
Mobile responsiveness: All 49 funnel pages must render correctly on mobile (iPhone and Android). This is a mobile-first funnel — 80%+ of traffic will come from phones via Instagra ---------- ta ads.
Survey redirect testing: Confirm all 4 conditional redirects route to the correct branch funnel.
Order form testing: Process test Stripe payments on all 8 order forms and verify post-purchase redirects.
Domain connection: Publish all funnels and connect to a custom domain (I will provide the domain and DNS access).

What I Will Provide
You will not be working from vague briefs. Everything is pre-built.
Complete build specification document (40+ pages): Full funnel architecture map, every page’s copy (word-for-word), element-by-element layout specs, colour palette with hex codes, typography direction, tag and custom field definitions, workflow logic, GHL AI Build prompts, Meta Pixel event map, and a phased build checklist.
GHL account access: Full admin access to the GHL sub-account where all assets will be built.
Stripe account access: Connected Stripe account for product creation and payment testing.
Brand assets: Logo files, any required imagery, and brand colour palette.
Domain and DNS access: For connecting published funnels to the live domain.
GHL AI Build prompts: Five ready-to-paste prompts for GHL’s Funnels AI feature to scaffold each page type. These accelerate initial page creation — you then refine, add order forms, embed surveys and calendars, and wire up workflows.
Ongoing availability: I will be available for questions, clarifications, and approvals throughout the build. Response time within 12 hours.
Required Skills & Experience
This is a hands-on GHL build engagement. I need someone who has built and shipped funnel systems inside GoHighLevel before — not someone learning on the job.
Must-Have
Proven GoHighLevel experience: You have built multi-step funnels, surveys, workflows, and order forms inside GHL on at least 3 previous projects. You can show examples or screenshots.
GHL Funnel Builder proficiency: You are comfortable with GHL’s section/row/column page builder, know how to add custom CSS, embed surveys, and configure two-step order forms.
GHL Survey & Conditional Logic: You have configured GHL surveys with conditional redirect rules and branching logic.
GHL Workflow Automation: You can build If/Else branching workflows with tag triggers, custom field updates, pipeline stage movements, and internal notifications.
Stripe integration: You have connected Stripe to GHL, created products, and configured two-step order forms with live payment processing.
Mobile-responsive design: You build mobile-first and test across devices. You understand that GHL’s desktop builder requires manual mobile adjustments.
Meta Pixel setup: You can install a Meta Pixel and fire event-specific triggers (Lead, Purchase, Schedule) at the correct funnel touchpoints.
Nice-to-Have
Experience with GHL’s Funnels & Websites AI (Beta) — the spec includes AI Build prompts that can accelerate scaffolding.
GHL Calendar configuration experience (appointment types, timezone detection, booking confirmation emails).
Familiarity with quiz funnel architecture (e.g., ---------- , ScoreApp, or similar quiz-to-offer flows).
Experience working from detailed specification documents rather than vague creative briefs.
Understanding of direct-response copywriting principles (not required to write copy, but helps with layout decisions).
Deliverables & Acceptance Criteria
The project is complete when all of the following are delivered and tested inside the GHL account:
1 quiz landing page with embedded GHL Survey, live and rendering correctly on mobile.
1 GHL Survey with 2 slides, 4 conditional redirect rules, and correct custom field mapping.
4 lead magnet funnels (4–5 steps each) with all copy implemented, all design tokens applied, and confetti scripts active on confirmation pages.
4 upsell funnels (3 steps each) with all copy, pricing, and checklists implemented.
4 coaching appointment funnels (4 steps each) with GHL Calendar embedded and branch-specific copy.
8 GHL two-step order forms connected to Stripe, processing test payments successfully.
5–8 GHL workflows with correct tag application, custom field updates, pipeline movements, and sequence triggers.
17 tags and 6 custom fields created and actively used by workflows.
2 GHL pipelines with correct stages.
Meta Pixel installed with event-specific triggers firing at all 6 defined touchpoints.
All funnels published and connected to the custom domain.
A full end-to-end test walk-through recorded on Loom (or equivalent) showing all 4 branch journeys working from quiz to coaching booking.
Timeline & Milestones
Estimated total duration: 10–14 working days. Milestones below are for progress check-ins and phased approval.

How to Apply

Important: This is not a design-from-scratch or copywriting project. ## **What You’ll Build**

* AI agents that fully automate **marketing, sales, content, operations, and client success**.

* Autonomous workflow for **lead scoring, personalized outreach, AI-driven calls, and conversion tracking**.
* Knowledge-sharing system to allow agents to **learn from each other and from client data**.
* Dashboard and reporting framework for entrepreneurs to **monitor and control their business at a glance**.

---

## **Why Join Us**

* Work on **cutting-edge AI technology** that could redefine entrepreneurship.
* Build a platform capable of scaling to **hundreds of thousands of clients worldwide**.
* Opportunity to shape the future of autonomous businesses.
* Competitive salary and the ability to work fully remote.
* Be part of a **visionary team building the “AWS for entrepreneurship”**.
